data.prompt:
You are Archon Horizon's Horizon agent. You turn the blueprints into checked Lean: you pick the most valuable next piece of formalization and carry it as far as you can in one session. You self-scope from the recommendation, roadmap, and opened inbox below.

You have broad freedom at the workspace level: experiment, change the strategy entirely, try alternative formulations, edit blueprint material when proving teaches you something, and — when a task calls for it — work across the several projects it spans, or create and merge projects. The one hard rule is hygiene: stay within the projects in scope and the shared `references/`, don't leave stray directories or scratch files behind. The Ground agent and its review subagents run after you to reconcile, check math/blueprint alignment, and tidy — so prefer making progress over being cautious.

How to work:
- Read the relevant blueprint node and proof sketch before touching Lean.
- Run the project's build/check command (or the narrowest useful Lean check) and repair failures you introduce. Don't bury obligations under new `sorry`s unless a hint allows it.
- Use the inbox to comment, close, create, and message other projects with `--author horizon` (see the horizon-inbox skill). ALWAYS write item bodies, comments, and reports in Markdown — short paragraphs and `-` bullet lists (never one wall-of-text paragraph), `**bold**` for the key claim, and backticks for ids, lemma names, and files. Give every item a short title and a non-empty description. Comment at each key advance, and add a concise closing comment (runs, LOC, files) before you `complete` an item. Treat `[persistent]` items as standing rules; close `[temporary]` ones once consumed.
- Record durable dead ends as memory: `horizon inbox add --kind memory --to horizon --author horizon --body "..."`, so later sessions don't repeat them.
- You may delegate to subagents when it helps (see the Subagents section): split a wide search across parallel read-only subagents, hand mechanical checks to a cheaper-model subagent, or pull references. You are free to decide whether they fit the task.

# Skills
Invoke the Horizon CLI via the `$HORIZON_BIN` env var (an absolute path), e.g. `"$HORIZON_BIN" inbox list --json` — a bare `horizon` may not be on your shell's PATH.
Most `horizon` commands accept `--json`; use it for AI-friendly output when reading state or ids.
Capability know-how lives in skill files under the workspace `.claude/skills/<name>/SKILL.md` (Claude Code surfaces them automatically; on other engines read the files directly). Consult the relevant one before acting (exact inbox verbs, lean checks, blueprint conventions, the dependency DAG):
- blueprint-conventions — The LaTeX-subset blueprint format and house style — environments, \uses/\lean/\leanok/\mathlibok/\notready, the math-purity rule, and complete (not sketched) proofs.
- horizon-inbox — Read and act on the Archon Horizon inbox — list/filter agent-ready items, understand labels/kinds, comment, close, create, and message other projects via the `horizon inbox` CLI.
- lean-check — Build and check Lean in this workspace and read the errors — use Lean LSP MCP for fast proof-writing feedback, then run faithful kernel verification with `lake build` / `lake env lean`.
- leandag — Consult and refresh the blueprint dependency DAG (leandag) — declaration dependencies, cones, proved status, isolated/dangling nodes, verified union/intersection across projects — via `horizon leandag` and the cached DAG files.
- leansearch — Find existing lemmas/definitions in mathlib, workspace projects, and configured libraries with `horizon search`; use external Mathlib search tools (LeanSearch/Loogle/Moogle/LSP) when broader Mathlib search is needed before writing proofs.
- project-git — How git works in an Archon Horizon workspace — root commits include scoped project files plus shared state, while each project also has an out-of-tree VCS journal for detailed per-round history.
- subagents — How the Ground agent delegates to native subagents to divide review/upkeep work — spawning by name, read-only enforcement, model tiers, and the dynamic descriptor roster.

Workspace: JacobianChallenge
Project: Albanese at SubProjects/Albanese
Write scope — you may edit ONLY paths matching these globs; treat everything else as read-only:
- SubProjects/Albanese/**
- references/**
Read scope: the entire workspace (cross-project reads are allowed).
Log/artifact directory: .archon-horizon/runs/0002/sessions/0013-horizon-T3

# Subagents
You have native subagents installed for your engine, compiled at run start from the descriptors in `.archon-horizon/subagents/<name>.md` (Claude reads `.claude/agents/`, Codex reads `.codex/agents/`):
- blueprint — Own one assigned blueprint slice end-to-end — keep it pure math with complete proofs, cite references with \source{...} anchors, hold a 1-to-1 correspondence with the Lean, and make its dependency cone finite and clean.
- debug — Diagnose and repair workspace setup/build/environment failures during a run — missing toolchain, broken lakefile, MCP/config/env problems, obscure non-Lean-syntax errors — applying safe fixes or reporting a precise diagnosis.
- janitor — Workspace-hygiene auditor and tidier — keeps the workspace and each subproject navigable, READMEs/roadmap concise and current, files findable, and the inbox from overflowing (capping memory tags, closing what's done).
- page-transcriber — Transcribe a small PDF page range (max 5 pages) into faithful per-page LaTeX using AI VISION on rendered page images (never OCR), each page self-contained with the operators/macros it declares.
- reference-retriever — Fetch original math references into workspace-root references/, register them in references/manifest.yaml, and when needed transcribe PDF page ranges into page-level LaTeX files for blueprint \source{...} anchors.
- work-reviewer [read-only] — Fresh-context review of the previous Horizon agent's work on a task — starts from its git diff, its report, and the task it was given, but is free to dig deeper — then judges whether the work is converging and files concise inbox issues/memory for stuck points and errors.
- blueprint-reviewer [read-only] — Check that the Lean declarations match their blueprint statements and the \uses dependency edges are correct.
- diff-auditor [read-only] — Audit the project diff from the last Horizon round for sound reasoning, no stray files, and no buried sorries.

Delegate by spawning a subagent **by name** through your engine's own subagent mechanism, giving it a focused directive: the slice/scope and the project it applies to. Spawn several in parallel when the work divides cleanly, then wait for and reconcile their reports. Read-only subagents are sandboxed off source edits and report through the `horizon inbox` CLI. You decide whether a subagent fits — use them when they help, skip them when they don't. Each subagent's descriptor pins its own model, usually a cheaper one for mechanical work: prefer delegating routine checks (diff review, reference lookup, lint) to those cheaper subagents and keep your own (more expensive) context for the hard reasoning. To add or change a subagent, edit its descriptor under `.archon-horizon/subagents/` — it recompiles on the next run.

# Latest Ground recommendation
This was saved as `recommendation.md` by the last Ground session; treat it as fresh guidance, not as a hard command if the live state contradicts it.
## Next
- Remaining Albanese sorries (~15) are all blocked/hard: `GmScaling.lean:770,941` (self-contained `𝔾ₘ`-scaling ring/`Fin` identities — best next target, iter-186 recipe in-file); `CodimOneExtension`/`Thm32` (Mathlib gaps 00TT/00OE/0AVF); `AlbaneseUP`/`FGA`/`WeilDivisor` (gated on `AJC.pic0av`/`AJC.picrep`/upstream).
- A future blueprint/leandag pass should resolve the `I-0014` private-tag caveat and refresh the DAG cache.

# Recommended focus
Task: T3
Title: Restore Albanese to sorry-free after the v4.31.0 bump
Project: Albanese
Suggested scope: projects=Albanese; files=AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/*,AlgebraicJacobian/Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/RigidityLemma.lean,AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/*

Restore **Albanese** to sorry-free after the mathlib **v4.31.0** bump.

The v4.31 migration reintroduced `sorry`s into the extracted Albanese leg (roadmap 2026-06-30: **17** open, comment-stripped over `AlgebraicJacobian/`). Repair the broken proofs so the project builds green again and can re-merge cleanly into AJC. These are mechanical migration debt, not new math gaps.

Sorries currently span:

- `Albanese/AlbaneseUP.lean`, `Albanese/AuslanderBuchsbaum.lean`, `Albanese/Thm32RationalMapExtension.lean`, `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`
- `Picard/FGAPicRepresentability.lean`, `RiemannRoch/WeilDivisor.lean`, `RigidityLemma.lean`
- `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean`, `Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean`, `Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean`

**Done when:** `lake build` is green with no new `sorry` or axioms; the roadmap Albanese row returns to 0.

# Roadmap slice
- ALB.ab [done/proof/normal] (Albanese) Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge - **Auslander–Buchsbaum / coheight bridge (sorry-free).** `AuslanderBuchsbaum`, `CoheightBridge`: the commutative-algebra input
$$\operatorname{depth}M+\operatorname{pd}M=\operatorname{depth}R$$
feeding codimension/coheight arguments for the extension theorems.

- ALB.codim1 [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension - **Codim-one & Thm 3.2 rational-map extension.** A rational map from a smooth variety to an abelian variety extends over codimension-$1$ points:
$$X\dashrightarrow A\ \Longrightarrow\ X\setminus Z\to A,\ \operatorname{codim}Z\ge 2.$$
`CodimOneExtension` (×3), `Thm32RationalMapExtension` (×2).

- ALB.fga [active/proof/normal] (Albanese) FGA Picard representability slice - **FGA Picard representability slice.** The `FGAPicRepresentability` portion carried in the Albanese extraction that feeds back into the AJC Picard cone.

- ALB.up [active/proof/high] (Albanese) Albanese universal property of Pic^0 - **Albanese universal property of $\mathrm{Pic}^{0}$** (Milne III §6, Prop 6.1). Every pointed map to an abelian variety factors uniquely through the Jacobian:
$$\forall\,\phi:(C,p)\to A,\ \exists!\ \bar{\phi}:\mathrm{Pic}^{0}_{C/k}\to A\ \text{with}\ \phi=\bar\phi\circ\mathrm{aj}.$$
`AlbaneseUP` (×7): the headline `Pic.albaneseUP` + universal-map descent.


# Opened inbox
- I-0008 hint (by=ground, to=horizon, labels=agent-ready, projects=Albanese): **Where to spend a T3 Albanese session — and where NOT to.** Albanese already **builds green** (only `sorry` warnings). Do not chase a global 0-sorry state; several sorries are blocked on other active roadmap cones. **Attack these (self-contained, no cross-project blocker):** - `Genus0BaseObjects/Points.lean:512` — `GeometricallyReduced (Gm kbar).hom` (𝔾ₘ geom. reduced). - `Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean:220` — ℙ¹ geometrically irreducible. - `Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean` (770, 821, 941) — ring/`Fin` identities on the 𝔾ₘ-scaling. - `Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean` (1348, 1630, 1698) — the codim-1 extension math (`ALB.codim1`). **Do NOT attempt (blocked — will waste the session):**…

# Memory
- v4.31.0 migration fix patterns (Cech + GR push-pull/pullback proofs)

Effective repairs for the **v4.31.0 defeq-transparency-wall** breaks in these projects:

- **Port from the AJC copy first.** `MainProjects/Algebraic-Jacobian-Challenge/.../<same file>` is often already sorry-free (e.g. `PresheafCech.lean`, `CechSectionIdentificationBase.lean`). `diff` the subproject vs AJC; if they differ only in the sorry regions, copy the AJC file wholesale.
- **`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in`** before a lemma fixes most "type mismatch under instances transparency" / stuck defeq. **Placement matters**: it must go BEFORE the docstring `/-- … -/`, never between docstring and `lemma` (that is a parse error: "unexpected token 'set_option'; expected 'lemma'"). Stacks with `set_option maxHeartbeats N in`.
- **`erw [Category.comp_id]` / `erw [Category.id_comp]`** closes residual `A ≫ 𝟙 = A` / `𝟙 ≫ A = A` when the `𝟙`'s object is only defeq (e.g. `(𝟭).obj M` vs `M`, or a `⋙`-form) so `simp`/`exact Category.comp_id _` fail. `erw` matches through the wall then auto-`rfl`.
- **`rw [reassoc_of% h1, reassoc_of% h2]` ordering**: cancel the INNER hom≫inv pair first so the outer pair becomes adjacent.
- **`subst h; simp <;> rfl`** for eqToHom-transport lemmas whose two sides reduce to defeq-but-not-syntactic `map = map`.
- **LSP is dead** on these full-`import Mathlib` files (server crashes / broken pipe). Iterate with `lake build <module>` and read the printed unsolved-goal state from the error output. `GlueDescent` ≈ 2.6 min/build; `CechHigherDirectImage` ≈ 8.5 min/build.
- **SOLVED (supersedes the earlier "two v4.31 sorrys remain hard" memory): both `rawPushPullMap_comp` (Cech) and `pullback_cast_compat` (GR) are now proved sorry-free.**

- **`CechHigherDirectImage.lean` `rawPushPullMap_comp`**: final transport is pure **term-mode**, no `rw` (avoids "motive not type correct"). Shape: `(congrArg (fun t => (pushforward p₁).map A_head ≫ t) he).trans (((Functor.map_comp _ _ _).symm.trans (congrArg (pushforward p₁).map ((Category.assoc _ _ _).trans INNER))).trans (Functor.map_comp _ _ _))`. `he` rewrites the outer over-triangle `eqToHom` to `(pushforward p₁).map eqInner`; `Category.assoc` fixes `(x≫y)≫z` vs `x≫(y≫z)` before `INNER`; `map_comp` folds/splits.

- **`GlueDescent.lean` `pullback_cast_compat`**: proved from the associativity solved-forms. Recipe: (1) `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` BEFORE the docstring (the `pullbackComp` solved-forms unfold to the `⋙`-object form; else v4.31 transparency wall blocks matching). (2) tagged `pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app` + `pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app` `@[reassoc]`. (3) phase-1 `simp only [pullbackComp_comp_fst_hom_app, pullback_map_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullback_map_congr_inv_comp_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_congr_hom_app_assoc, pullbackComp_inv_comp_map_inv_app(_assoc), Category.assoc]` keeping congr as casts. (4) phase-2 `simp only [pullbackCongr_hom/inv_app_eqToHom, eqToHom_trans, eqToHom_trans_assoc, eqToHom_refl, Category.assoc, comp_id, id_comp]` (need `eqToHom_trans_assoc` for mid-chain). Variance: `pullbackComp x y : pullback y ⋙ pullback x ≅ pullback (x≫y)`.

- Iterate via `lake build <module>` reading the unsolved-goal state (LSP dead).
- Recipes: closing GeometricallyReduced / GeometricallyIrreducible scaffold sorries (v4.31 Mathlib has the full API)

Mathlib v4.31 ships `AlgebraicGeometry/Geometrically/{Basic,Reduced,Irreducible}.lean` — these scaffold instances are now provable, not gaps. Proven patterns (see `Points.lean` `gm_geometricallyReduced`, `ChartIso.lean` `projectiveLineBar_geomIrred`, both axiom-clean):

- **Affine case** (`Spec A ⟶ Spec k̄`): `rw [geometricallyReduced_iff / geometricallyIrreducible_iff]`, intro the `IsPullback`, `obtain ⟨φ, rfl⟩ := Spec.map_surjective y; algebraize [φ.hom]`, then `h.isoPullback ≪≫ pullbackSpecIso k̄ A K` reduces to `IsReduced/IsDomain (A ⊗[k̄] K)`. Needs `set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in` (the `(Gm k̄).hom`-to-`Spec.map` defeq).
- **Ring step for localizations**: `IsReduced (Localization.Away t ⊗[k̄] K)` via `Algebra.TensorProduct.cancelBaseChange k̄ P P _ K` (`≅ Loc ⊗[P] (P ⊗[k̄] K)`), instance `IsLocalization.tensor`, and `isReduced_localizationPreserves` (apply with `by exact` + explicit S — beta-reduction quirk). `MvPolynomial.algebraTensorAlgEquiv` + `Algebra.TensorProduct.comm` give `P ⊗[k̄] K ≅ MvPolynomial σ K`.
- **Proj / non-affine case** (ℙ¹): cover the base change by pulled-back chart opens. Per chart: `Proj.awayι_toSpecZero_assoc` + `← Spec.map_comp` + `change` collapses `awayι ≫ hom` to `Spec.map (algebraMap k̄ (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`, then the affine recipe. Covering: `Proj.iSup_basicOpen_eq_top` with the (now named) `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_span`. Intersection: any preimage of the generic point `⟨⊥, Ideal.isPrime_bot, …⟩` under `pullback.fst` (surjective — `Surjective` is `IsStableUnderBaseChange`, `MorphismProperty.pullback_fst`). Assemble with the new `irreducibleSpace_of_two_irreducible_opens` (ChartIso).
- **Traps**: inside `namespace AlgebraicGeometry`, ring-level `IsReduced` must be `_root_.IsReduced`; `⊗[k̄]` notation needs `open scoped TensorProduct in` BEFORE the docstring; avoid `ObjectProperty.prop_of_iso _` (leaves an unsynthesizable eta-expanded `IsClosedUnderIsomorphisms` goal — use `isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (iso).hom` or `.homeomorph.irreducibleSpace_iff` instead); the affine cover's `I₀`/`𝒰.f i` are semireducibly opaque — work with `Proj.awayι (X i)` + `fin_cases` normalization (`projectiveLineBar_smooth_chart_aux` idiom) rather than cover indices.

This is a ONE-SHOT headless session: when you produce your final report the session ENDS and you will NOT be re-invoked. Nothing calls you back when a background job finishes, and any background process you started is killed when the session exits. So you cannot 'kick off a build and wait to be notified' — that notification never comes here, and the build dies.
Therefore: if a build, subagent, monitor, or shell command produces a result your conclusion depends on, you MUST get that result WITHIN this session — run it in the FOREGROUND and block on it (read its exit code), or actively poll until it has definitively finished, before writing the report. A heavy `lake build` can take many minutes; budget for that and wait it out. If it genuinely cannot finish in time, do NOT imply success: record the unfinished build under `## Issues`, state what's still unknown, and leave the explicit next action — never write a conclusion that assumes a pending result.

Finish with a brief run-local report. Recommended sections: `## Summary`, `## Progress`, `## Issues`, and `## Next`; add or rename sections if another shape is clearer. Prefer short bullets, and keep each bullet around 20 words or fewer. Always mention bugs, build failures, broken proofs, suspicious code issues, blocked dependencies, unresolved assumptions, and checks that failed or were not run. For this Horizon session, make the report complete enough that a human or Ground can understand the session before opening raw logs.

1	# Analogy: cover-vs-`Proj.awayι` syntactic mismatch in `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
2	
3	## Mode
4	api-alignment
5	
6	## Slug

12	## Question
13	What Mathlib idiom retires the two TEMPORARY axioms
14	`gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp` and `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero_temp` in
15	`AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean` (iter-177 HARD STOP
16	corrective)? Specifically: how to rewrite `cover.openCover.f i` back to
17	`Proj.awayι _ (X_i) _ _` form so the chart-bridge proof body chasing
18	`pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` succeeds.

23	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:120-160` — `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (the blocker; `match i with | ⟨0, _⟩ => … | ⟨1, _⟩ => …`).
24	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:175-194` — `gmScalingP1_chart` (axiom-clean def; uses the iso).
25	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:212-219` — `gmScalingP1_chart_data_temp` (TEMPORARY AXIOM, to be retired).
26	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/GmScaling.lean:308-311` — `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero_temp` (TEMPORARY AXIOM, to be retired).
27	- `AlgebraicJacobian/Genus0BaseObjects/BareScheme.lean:175-221` — `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` via `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` (the cover whose `.f i` we need to identify with `Proj.awayι _ (X i) _ _`).
28	
29	## Decisions identified
30	
31	### Decision 1: Is `Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover_f` + `Matrix.cons_val` the right idiom?
32	
33	- **Mathlib idiom**: PARTIAL — `Scheme.AffineOpenCover.openCover_f`
34	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open`) gives `𝒰.openCover.f j = 𝒰.f j`
35	  by `rfl`. For `𝒰 = Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan 𝒜 f f_deg hm hf`,
36	  the field `.f i` is **definitionally** `Proj.awayι 𝒜 (f i) (f_deg i) (hm i)`
37	  (verified: generic `i`-form is `rfl` after a `let cov := …` introduces the
38	  cover and `f_deg`/`hm` are bound names, not inline `by` tactics).
39	- **Subtle structural blocker**: when the index is written `⟨0, _⟩`
40	  (NOT canonical `(0 : Fin 2)`), Lean's elaborator does NOT reduce
41	  `(![X 0, X 1]) ⟨0, _⟩` to `X 0`. Concretely verified: `f_deg ⟨0, by omega⟩ = hf₀`
42	  fails with a Type mismatch error, while `f_deg (0 : Fin 2) = hf₀` is `rfl`.
43	  The reason: `⟨0, h⟩` carries a *metavariable* proof at elaboration time;
44	  the elaborator never instantiates the `Fin.cons`/`Matrix.cons` computation
45	  rule unless the index normalizes to a canonical `Fin.OfNat` form.
46	- **Compounding factor**: when `f_deg`/`hm` are built inline via
47	  `(fun i => by fin_cases i <;> simp [...])`, the kernel must `whnf` the
48	  tactic-built proof terms in addition to the index reduction, which times
49	  out at 200k heartbeats (verified) and even at 800k (verified).
50	- **Project's current path**: `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` matches on `i`
51	  via `match i with | ⟨0, _⟩ => … | ⟨1, _⟩ => …`. In each branch, the body
52	  references `MvPolynomial.X 0` / `MvPolynomial.X 1` directly — but the
53	  target type uses `MvPolynomial.X i`. The match equation `i = ⟨0, _⟩`
54	  doesn't trigger the `Matrix.cons_val_zero` reduction needed to unify
55	  `(cover.openCover.f i = Proj.awayι 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) ⟨0, _⟩) …)` with
56	  `(Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X 0) …)` inside `pullback.congrHom`.
57	- **Gap**: divergent-with-cost. Mathlib has the idiom (`openCover_f` is
58	  the right path), but the project's `match-on-i` decoration of
59	  `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` causes the iso to be **specialised** in each
60	  branch in a way that breaks the `Matrix.cons` defeq chain.
61	- **Cost of the current divergence**: 6 iters of helper-bridge attempts
62	  (iter-172 → iter-177) failed to penetrate. iter-177 HARD STOP corrective
63	  admitted two project axioms.
64	- **Verdict**: **ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB** — refactor `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`
65	  to be **uniform in `i`** (no `match`), so the bridge chain works
66	  generically and the `Matrix.cons_val` reduction happens INSIDE the cover's
67	  rfl-defeq, not at the `pullback.congrHom` boundary.
68	
69	### Decision 2: Does Mathlib ship a `Proj.openCover` / `Proj.coverByCharts` that bypasses the issue?
70	
71	- **Mathlib idiom**: PARTIAL.
72	  - `Proj.affineOpenCover` (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic`):
73	    uses ALL homogeneous-degree-positive elements (not Fin-indexed); too coarse
74	    for the project's 2-chart setting.
75	  - `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` (already used by the project):
76	    Fin-indexed, `.f i = Proj.awayι 𝒜 (f i) (f_deg i) (hm i)` by `rfl`.
77	  - `Proj.openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop` / `Proj.openCoverOfMapIrreleventEqTop`:
78	    different shape (covers `X` from a `RingHom A → Γ(X, ⊤)`), not applicable.
79	- **Project's current path**: uses `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan`,
80	  which IS the right Mathlib idiom — the structural issue is downstream
81	  (the `match` in `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`), not the cover choice itself.
82	- **Gap**: identical (project uses the canonical Mathlib idiom).
83	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** — no upstream refactor needed; the cover itself is
84	  Mathlib-aligned.
85

99	      pullback.congrHom
100	        (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar
101	          ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
102	          (-- hoist this f_deg proof to a top-level `noncomputable def` in BareScheme.lean
103	           projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
104	        (show (Gm kbar).hom =
105	            Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
106	      pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
107	  ```
108	  Key structural change: the iso target uses `((![X 0, X 1]) i)` instead of
109	  `(MvPolynomial.X i)`, eliminating the defeq pivot. Downstream, `chart_PLB_eq`
110	  does a fresh `fin_cases i` AFTER unfolding, with `Matrix.cons_val_zero`/`one`
111	  closing the chart-ring iso target — but now the bridge chain is uniform in
112	  `i`, so `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` applies cleanly.
113	- **Required companion definition** (in `BareScheme.lean`, ~5-8 LOC):
114	  hoist the `f_deg : ∀ i, (![X 0, X 1]) i ∈ 𝒜 1` proof from the inline `by`
115	  tactic in `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` (currently `(fun i => by fin_cases i <;> simp [...])`)
116	  to a named `noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`. The
117	  `hm : ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1]) i` similarly hoisted to
118	  `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm`. This makes the proof terms reduce
119	  cleanly without the kernel chasing tactic-built proof closures.
120	- **LOC delta estimate**:
121	  - Hoisted `f_deg`/`hm` defs in `BareScheme.lean`: ~12 LOC.
122	  - `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` body refactor (eliminate `match`): -20, +12 ⟹ net ~-8 LOC.
123	  - `gmScalingP1_chart` body: minor signature update for the iso target; ~+5 LOC.
124	  - `gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq` body retirement (was axiom, now proven): ~+35-50 LOC
125	    using the 10-step recipe from `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3.
126	  - `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement` body retirement: ~+30-50 LOC
127	    (Sub-task B from `chart-bridge-shared-helper.md`).
128	  - `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body retirement: ~+15-25 LOC
129	    (chart-1 ring-map computation; the chart-1 ring map sends `u ↦ u ⊗ λ`,
130	    and `zeroPt` factors through chart-1 at `u = 0`, so the composite
131	    evaluates to `zeroPt` independently of `λ`).
132	- **Total**: ~85-130 LOC across 5 lanes (within iter-177's ~80-150 estimate).
133	- **Verdict**: **PROCEED** with the uniform-in-`i` refactor.
134	
135	### Decision 4: Alternative — valuative-criterion route to `ℙ¹ → A const` (bypass chart-bridge)
136	
137	- **Mathlib idiom**: PARTIAL.
138	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.IsProper.of_valuativeCriterion`
139	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ValuativeCriterion`): proves properness FROM
140	    the valuative criterion. We need the converse direction (use properness
141	    to extend a morphism), accessed via `ValuativeCriterion.existence`
142	    (extracts the existence half from `[IsProper]`).
143	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.RationalMap` / `Scheme.PartialMap`
144	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.RationalMap`): framework for partial
145	    morphisms with `domain : X.Opens` and `dense_domain`. Has the SHAPE
146	    needed.
147	  - `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.valuativeCriterion_existence_aux`
148	    (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Proper`): proves the
149	    existence half OF the criterion FOR `Proj` (used in the project's
150	    `projectiveLineBar_isProper` derivation chain); not the converse.
151	- **What's MISSING in Mathlib**: there is no single shipped lemma of the
152	  form "if `f : U ⟶ Y` is a morphism from a dense open of a regular
153	  Noetherian scheme `X` to a proper scheme `Y`, and `X \ U` has codimension
154	  ≥ 1, then `f` extends uniquely to a morphism `X ⟶ Y`". This is
155	  exactly the content of Route A's `A.4.a Lemma 3.3 codim-1` lane
156	  (`AlgebraicJacobian/Albanese/CodimOneExtension.lean`, currently skeleton-only,
157	  Mathlib gap CRITICAL PATH per `STRATEGY.md`).
158	- **Project's current path**: chart-bridge route (option (c) Route C from
159	  iter-170). The valuative-criterion alternative was tracked as a
160	  pre-committed replacement candidate (STRATEGY.md Open Q) gated on
161	  iter-182 status review.
162	- **Gap**: divergent-and-wrong (the valuative-criterion alternative). The
163	  alternative requires building essentially the SAME infrastructure as
164	  Route A.4.a (which is the dominant positive-genus risk; substrate UNOWNED).
165	  For a genus-0 sub-case (extending `𝔸¹ → A` to `ℙ¹ → A`), the codim-1
166	  extension is a single DVR lift, not the full surface-divisor machinery
167	  Route A needs — but the project would still need:
168	    (i) Build `Scheme.PartialMap`-to-`Scheme.Hom` extension across a closed
169	        codim-1 reduced subscheme (here `{∞} ⊂ ℙ¹`),
170	    (ii) Apply `ValuativeCriterion.existence` (extracted from `[IsProper A]`)
171	         to the localised DVR `O_{ℙ¹, ∞}`, then
172	    (iii) Glue back to a morphism `ℙ¹ ⟶ A` (essentially
173	          `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms` of the affine chart + a Spec-of-DVR
174	          patch at `∞`).
175	  Step (i) is genuinely new project material; step (iii) is the SAME
176	  `Cover.glueMorphisms` blocker that's biting the chart-bridge route.
177	- **Cost of the valuative alternative**: ~80-130 LOC for steps (i)-(iii)
178	  PLUS the same `glueMorphisms` infrastructure cost (which the chart-bridge
179	  refactor already pays for). NET: no savings, plus a fresh
180	  `RationalMap`-extension lemma that's not Mathlib-shipped.
181	- **Verdict**: **DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY** — stay on the chart-bridge route
182	  (Decisions 1-3 are the path). The valuative alternative was a sensible
183	  backup but does not eliminate the structural blocker; it just shifts it.

185	## Verdicts (summary)
186	
187	| Decision | Verdict | Severity |
188	|---|---|---|
189	| (1) `openCover_f` + `Matrix.cons_val` recipe, but `match`-on-i defeats it | ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB | critical |
190	| (2) `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` is the right cover; project already uses it | PROCEED | informational |
191	| (3) Uniform-in-`i` refactor of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (~85-130 LOC) | PROCEED | informational |
192	| (4) Valuative-criterion alternative: no savings, shifts same blocker | DIVERGE_INTENTIONALLY | informational |
193	
194	## Recommendation
195	
196	**Land the uniform-in-`i` refactor of `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso`**
197	(Decisions 1 + 3). The two TEMPORARY axioms retire via a 3-step refactor:
198	
199	### Step 1 — Hoist tactic-built proof terms in `BareScheme.lean` (~12 LOC)
200	
201	Add two top-level named defs that replace the inline `by` tactics in
202	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover`:
203	
204	```lean
205	private noncomputable def projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg
206	    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
207	    ∀ i, (![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1]) i ∈
208	      projectiveLineBarGrading kbar ((![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i) :=
209	  fun i => by
210	    fin_cases i <;> simp [Matrix.cons_val_zero, Matrix.cons_val_one,
211	      MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X]
212	
213	private lemma projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm :
214	    ∀ i, 0 < (![1, 1] : Fin 2 → ℕ) i :=
215	  fun i => by fin_cases i <;> exact Nat.one_pos
216	```
217	
218	Then refactor `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` to consume these by name
219	(instead of inline `by` tactics).
220	
221	### Step 2 — Refactor `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` uniform in `i` (~ -8 LOC net)
222	
223	Replace the `match i with | ⟨0, _⟩ => … | ⟨1, _⟩ => …` body with a
224	single uniform expression whose target type uses
225	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` instead of `(MvPolynomial.X i)`. Pass the
226	hoisted `projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i` as the
227	homogeneity witness to `awayι_comp_PLB_hom`. The post-`congrHom`
228	pullback is now uniformly
229	`pullback (Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 (![X 0, X 1] i)))) (Gm.hom)`,
230	so `pullbackSpecIso kbar _ _` applies generically.
231	
232	### Step 3 — Retire the two temp axioms (~80-125 LOC across 3 bodies)
233	
234	1. **`gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq`** (currently axiom-laundered):
235	   follow the 10-step recipe in `analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md`
236	   Decision 3 (steps 1-10 listed there with verbatim Mathlib citations).
237	   With the uniform iso from Step 2, `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` fires
238	   syntactically and the bridge chain closes generically.
239	
240	2. **`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement`** (currently axiom-laundered, second
241	   conjunct): follow `chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Sub-task B —
242	   diagonal cases via `CategoryTheory.Limits.fst_eq_snd_of_mono_eq`
243	   (since `(cover).f i` is `IsOpenImmersion`, hence mono); cross cases
244	   `(0,1)/(1,0)` via the algebraic identity `λ·u = (1/t)·λ` in
245	   `Localization.Away t ⊗[kbar] GmRing` (per `analogies/gmscaling-deep.md` Q4).
246	
247	3. **`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero`** (currently axiom-laundered): once
248	   `gmScalingP1` is concrete, the chart-1 ring map sends `u ↦ u ⊗ λ` and
249	   `zeroPt` factors through chart-1 at `u = 0`, so the composite evaluates
250	   to `zeroPt` independently of `λ`. The proof chain: unfold
251	   `gmScalingP1`, apply `Cover.hom_ext` on the `cover` of
252	   `(ℙ¹ ⊗ Gm).left`, reduce on chart-1 to a ring-level identity in
253	   `MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing` (sending the constant `0`
254	   under `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom`'s `X ()`-binding).
255	
256	### Reversal trigger
257	
258	If Step 2 cannot eliminate the `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` syntactic
259	mismatch even with the uniform-in-`i` refactor (e.g., the
260	`MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing` chart-ring-iso's kbar-algebra
261	preservation surfaces a fresh unification issue), iter-179 falls back
262	to the **partial `Fin.cases` refactor**: keep the `match` in
263	`gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` but use `Fin.cases` (which produces the
264	canonical `(0 : Fin 2)` index, not `⟨0, _⟩`) — see the experimental
265	finding below for verification that `(0 : Fin 2)` triggers the right
266	`Matrix.cons_val` reduction.
267	
268	## Experimental verification (iter-178)
269	
270	The diagnosis above was empirically verified via four `lean_run_code`
271	probes:
272	
273	1. **Generic `i`-form is rfl-defeq**: when `f_deg`/`hm` are named
274	   hypotheses (not inline `by` tactics) and the index is left generic,
275	   `cov.openCover.f i = Proj.awayι 𝒜 ((![f₀, f₁]) i) (f_deg i) (hm i)`
276	   closes by `rfl`. ✓
277	2. **Canonical `0 : Fin 2` form is rfl-defeq**: when `f_deg` is a named
278	   hypothesis and the index is `(0 : Fin 2)`,
279	   `cov.openCover.f (0 : Fin 2) = Proj.awayι 𝒜 f₀ hf₀ Nat.one_pos`
280	   closes by `rfl` (proof irrelevance handles the proof-term mismatch
281	   between `hf₀` and `f_deg 0`). ✓
282	3. **`⟨0, _⟩` form fails**: when the same goal uses `⟨0, by omega⟩` as
283	   the index, `rfl` times out at 800k heartbeats. The elaborator does
284	   NOT unfold `(![f₀, f₁]) ⟨0, _⟩` to `f₀`. ✗
285	4. **Inline `by`-tactic in cover construction compounds the failure**:
286	   when `f_deg`/`hm` are built inline via `(fun i => by fin_cases i <;> ...)`,
287	   even Step 2's `simp [openCover_f, Fin.mk_zero', Matrix.cons_val_zero]`
288	   followed by `rfl` times out. ✗
289	
290	The combination of (3) + (4) is precisely why the iter-176 analogist's
291	`simp [Fin.isValue, Fin.zero_eta]` recipe failed: the simp set normalized
292	the Fin indexing but didn't reach into the `f_deg`/`hm` tactic closures.
293

300	  consume in Step 2).

302	  refactored `gmScalingP1_chart`).

35	### Decision 1: `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalization_mul` is in Mathlib and is the right idiom
36

38	  (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/RingTheory/GradedAlgebra/HomogeneousLocalization.lean:883`)
39	  packages: for `f ∈ 𝒜 d` (d ≠ 0) and `g ∈ 𝒜 e` and `x = f * g`,
40	  `Away 𝒜 x` is the localization of `Away 𝒜 f` at the image
41	  `Away.isLocalizationElem hf hg = g^d / f^e ∈ Away 𝒜 f` (L877).
42	- **Project's current path**: the iter-181 Lane B task_result correctly
43	  identified this lemma (lines 67–68 of the report) but treated it as a
44	  stand-alone Mathlib bridge to be threaded by hand.

47	  helper is needed at the ring level — Decision 2 supersedes the ring
48	  step entirely.

50	### Decision 2 (the load-bearing one): Mathlib ALREADY ships the Spec-level intersection iso `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`
51	
52	- **Mathlib idiom**: `AlgebraicGeometry.Proj.pullbackAwayιIso`
53	  (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/AlgebraicGeometry/ProjectiveSpectrum/Basic.lean:258-265`)
54	  is verbatim:
55	
56	  ```lean

58	  def pullbackAwayιIso :
59	      Limits.pullback (awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm) (awayι 𝒜 g g_deg hm') ≅
60	        Spec (.of <| Away 𝒜 x) :=  -- where hx : x = f * g
61	    IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq ...
62	  ```
63	
64	  Companion `reassoc (attr := simp)` lemmas (L268-302):
65	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι` (L268): `iso.hom ≫ awayι 𝒜 x _ _ =
66	    pullback.fst _ _ ≫ awayι 𝒜 f f_deg hm`.

69	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_hom_SpecMap_awayMap_right` (L282): `iso.hom ≫
70	    Spec.map (ofHom (awayMap 𝒜 f_deg ...)) = pullback.snd _ _`.
71	  - `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst` / `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd` (L291-301):

101	### Decision 3: Pasting from inner to outer pullback uses generic Mathlib limit-pasting
102	
103	- **Setup**: the cover `gmScalingP1_cover = (projectiveLineBarAffineCover).openCover.pullback₁ q`
104	  where `q := pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom : (ℙ¹⊗Gm).left ⟶ PLB.left`. By

112	  1. `pullback (pullback.fst q A) (pullback.fst q B) ≅ pullback q (pullback A B)`

131	    ≅ Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`. ~50-60 LOC; the

134	### Decision 4: chart-1 section for `iotaGm_range_isOpen` — `pullback.lift` over `pointOfVec`'s factorization through `awayι X_1`
135	
136	- **Mathlib idiom**: `Over.lift_left`
137	  (`.lake/packages/mathlib/Mathlib/CategoryTheory/LocallyCartesianClosed/Over.lean:130-131`):
138	  `(CartesianMonoidalCategory.lift f g).left = pullback.lift f.left g.left _`.
139	  Combined with `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd`, this expresses
140	  `(lift (toUnit ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm)).left` as the pullback-universal-property
141	  morphism into `pullback PLB.hom Gm.hom = (ℙ¹⊗Gm).left`.
142	
143	- **Section into chart-1 of cover**: `ProjectiveLineBar.onePt = pointOfVec
144	  kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 _`. `pointOfVec` is `Over.homMk
145	  (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
146	  (evalIntoGlobal kbar v) ...)`. The `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections`
147	  morphism factors through ANY chart of `Proj 𝒜` where the corresponding
148	  generator is mapped to a unit — for `onePt` with `v 0 = v 1 = 1`, BOTH
149	  `X_0 ↦ 1` and `X_1 ↦ 1` are units, so `onePt.left` factors through
150	  `awayι 𝒜 (X 1) _ _` via a section `r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec(Away 𝒜 (X 1))`
151	  (= `Spec.map` of the chart-1 ring's eval-at-1 map). The Mathlib
152	  factorization lemma is `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`
153	  (cited by the iter-168 `gmscaling-deep.md` analogist).
154	
155	- **Section formula**:
156	  ```
157	  s : Gm.left ⟶ (cover).X 1 = pullback q (awayι X_1)
158	  s := pullback.lift ((toUnit Gm ≫ onePt).left ≫ r_1.inv) (𝟙 Gm.left ≫ Gm.hom...) _
159	       -- actually: pullback.lift (Gm.left ⟶ Spec(Away X_1)) (Gm.left ⟶ Gm.left)
160	       --   with compatibility coming from the over-structure
161	  ```
162	
163	  Concretely: extract `r_1 := Spec.map (ofHom (the eval map Away 𝒜 (X 1) → k̄))`
164	  via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` on the chart-1 basic open,
165	  then `pullback.lift (toUnit_Gm ≫ r_1) (𝟙 Gm.left) (pullback compatibility)`.
166	
167	- **Open-immersion argument**: with `s` in hand,
168	  `s ≫ (cover).f 1 ≫ gmScalingP1.left = (lift _ _ ≫ gmScalingP1).left`
169	  by `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms` and unfolding of `gmScalingP1 = Over.homMk
170	  (cover.glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart _)`. The composite
171	  `s ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1` is the chart-1 morphism `(cover).X 1 ⟶
172	  ProjectiveLineBarScheme` precomposed with the section `s`. By
173	  unfolding `gmScalingP1_chart 1` (L156–175) and using
174	  `Proj.awayι` being an `IsOpenImmersion` instance
175	  (`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.ProjectiveSpectrum.Basic:196`), the
176	  composite is the open immersion `Gm = Spec k̄[t, t⁻¹] ↪ ℙ¹` mapping
177	  `λ ↦ [λ : 1]`. `IsOpenImmersion.isOpen_range` closes the helper.
178	
179	- **Gap**: BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER (no single Mathlib lemma exposes the
180	  section formula generically; this is a project-specific computation).
181	- **Verdict**: **BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER** for the chart-1 section `s`.
182	
183	## Mathlib API citations (verified at iter-182)

186	match the project's pinned `lake-manifest.json` commit:

207	- `Scheme.Cover.pullback₁` (the AG-side wrapper) and its `pullback₁_X` /
208	  `pullback₁_f` simp lemmas — `AlgebraicGeometry/Cover/MorphismProperty.lean:177`
209	  and (via reindexing) the bridge lemma in `Cover/Open.lean:160-180`
210	- `Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`, `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms`,

219	
220	| Sub-task | LOC | Mathlib idiom drives most of it? |
221	|---|---|---|
222	| `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` helper | 50–60 | YES (pasting iso chain) |
223	| `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` body | 30–45 | YES (Spec.map + ring algebra) |
224	| `iotaGm_range_isOpen` body — section + open immersion | 45–60 | PARTIAL (section construction is project-side; open-immersion conclusion is Mathlib) |
225	| `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` body (BONUS — same techniques) | 30–40 | YES (chart-1 ring map; `pullback.lift` factoring through `r_0`) |

227	**Total**: ~155–205 LOC across 3 (or 4 with the bonus) bodies. The
228	iter-181 task_result's 70–115 LOC estimate was too low because it
229	didn't budget the pasting iso step; on the other hand, the iter-181
230	"build intersection ring iso explicitly" line evaporates (Mathlib

273	               (![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] : _) 1 = _)).hom_awayι.symm) ≪≫

293	### Recipe 2: `gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` body
294	
295	```lean
296	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01 (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
297	    pullback.fst ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (0 : Fin 2))

299	      gmScalingP1_chart kbar (0 : Fin 2) =

301	        ((gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f (1 : Fin 2)) ≫
302	      gmScalingP1_chart kbar (1 : Fin 2) := by
303	  -- Promote both sides to the intersection ring via the new iso.
304	  rw [← cancel_epi (gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso kbar).inv]
305	  -- Each side now is `iso.inv ≫ pullback.fst ≫ chart 0` (resp. snd ≫ chart 1).
306	  -- Use `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst` / `_inv_snd` (composed through the outer iso)
307	  -- to rewrite both sides into a single Spec.map of a ring map
308	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X 0) ⊗ GmRing →+* Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1) ⊗ GmRing` and
309	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X 1) ⊗ GmRing →+* Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1) ⊗ GmRing`.
310	  simp only [Iso.inv_hom_id_assoc, Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst,
311	    Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd, ...]
312	  -- Goal now: equality of two Spec.map's. Reduce to ring equality.
313	  rw [← Spec.map_comp, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
314	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
315	  congr 1
316	  apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
317	  -- Ring-level identity: `chart 0`-side and `chart 1`-side ring maps
318	  -- agree on `Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1) ⊗ GmRing`. Via `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext`
319	  -- (after passing through `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso`), reduce to
320	  -- evaluating on the chart-side generators `t = X 1 / X 0` (chart 0) /
321	  -- `u = X 0 / X 1` (chart 1). The compatibility `t · u = 1` in
322	  -- `Localization.Away (X 0 · X 1)` is `IsLocalization.Away.mul_invSelf`
323	  -- applied to the merged isLocalizationElem.
324	  ext  -- or `MvPolynomial.algHom_ext` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_X'`
325	  -- residual algebra: `t · u = 1` (chart-product) — close via
326	  -- `Algebra.TensorProduct.tmul_mul_tmul` + `IsLocalization.Away.mul_invSelf`.
327	  sorry  -- target: ~10 LOC

337	  --    (by Over-pullback compatibility)`, via `Over.lift_left`.
338	  rw [Over.lift_left]  -- or use the `set_option` trick if defeq is tricky
339	  -- (b) `onePt.left` factors through `awayι X_1` via `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict`.
340	  --    Extract the section `r_1 : Spec k̄ ⟶ Spec (Away 𝒜 (X 1))`.
341	  obtain ⟨r_1, h_r_1⟩ : ∃ r_1 : Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar) ⟶
342	      Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
343	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X 1))),
344	      (ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar).left =
345	        r_1 ≫ Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
346	          (MvPolynomial.X 1) (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar 1) Nat.one_pos := by
347	    -- Use `Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` on the chart-1 basic open
348	    -- and the fact that `evalIntoGlobal kbar (fun _ => 1)` maps `X_1 ↦ 1`
349	    -- which is a unit (so the morphism restricts to the chart).
350	    sorry  -- ~15 LOC; chart factorization via Mathlib's chart-restriction lemma
351	  -- (c) Build the section `s : Gm.left ⟶ (cover).X 1` via `pullback.lift`.
352	  --    `(cover).X 1 = pullback q (awayι X_1)` with `q = pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom`.
353	  --    The Gm.left-side map is `toUnit_Gm ≫ r_1`; the Gm.hom-side is `𝟙 Gm.left`.
354	  set s : (Gm kbar).left ⟶ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X 1 := pullback.lift
355	    (pullback.lift ((Gm kbar).hom ≫ ... -- the PLB-side factor: factors through r_1)
356	      (𝟙 (Gm kbar).left) (...) )
357	    (𝟙 (Gm kbar).left)
358	    (...) with hs_def
359	  -- (d) `s ≫ (cover).f 1 = (lift (toUnit ≫ onePt) (𝟙 Gm)).left` — pullback unique-lift.
360	  have hs_fact : s ≫ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f 1 =
361	      (lift (toUnit (Gm kbar) ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.onePt kbar)
362	        (𝟙 (Gm kbar))).left := by
363	    -- unfold `s`, apply `pullback.lift_fst`, then `Over.lift_left` rev.
364	    sorry  -- ~5 LOC
365	  -- (e) Compose with `gmScalingP1.left = (cover).glueMorphisms gmScalingP1_chart _`.
366	  --    `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms` gives `(cover).f 1 ≫ glueMorphisms ... = gmScalingP1_chart 1`.
367	  rw [← hs_fact, Category.assoc]
368	  change IsOpen (Set.range ⇑(s ≫ (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f 1 ≫ (gmScalingP1 kbar).left))
369	  change IsOpen (Set.range ⇑(s ≫ Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms ... -- unfold to gmScalingP1_chart 1
370	  rw [Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms (gmScalingP1_cover kbar) _ _ 1]
371	  -- (f) `s ≫ gmScalingP1_chart 1` is an `IsOpenImmersion`:
372	  --     the chart map factors as `s ≫ iso.hom ≫ Spec.map (ring map) ≫ Proj.awayι X_1`.

374	  --     open immersion iff the ring map exhibits a localization. For the chart-1 ring map

380	## Verification (lean_run_code probe at iter-182)
381	
382	```lean
383	-- Probe: confirm `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` types as advertised.
384	example {A : Type*} [CommRing A] {σ : Type*} [SetLike σ A] [AddSubgroupClass σ A]
385	    {𝒜 : ℕ → σ} [GradedRing 𝒜]
386	    {m m' : ℕ} {f : A} (hf : f ∈ 𝒜 m) (hm : 0 < m)
387	    {g : A} (hg : g ∈ 𝒜 m') (hm' : 0 < m')
388	    {x : A} (hx : x = f * g) :
389	    Limits.pullback (Proj.awayι 𝒜 f hf hm) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 g hg hm') ≅
390	      Spec (.of <| HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 x) :=
391	  Proj.pullbackAwayιIso 𝒜 hf hm hg hm' hx
392	-- Result at iter-182: elaborates cleanly (the only non-trivial typeclass is
393	-- `SetLike σ A`, present at the cover's call site via `projectiveLineBarGrading`'s
394	-- standard ℕ-grading instances).
395	```
396	
397	## Verdicts (summary)
398	
399	| Decision | Verdict | Severity |
400	|---|---|---|
401	| (1) `HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalization_mul` is the right ring-level idiom | PROCEED | informational |
402	| (2) `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` IS shipped — discard "build intersection ring iso" plan | ALIGN_WITH_MATHLIB | critical |
403	| (3) Outer "intersection cover X iso" still needed; pasting via Mathlib idioms | BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER | informational |
404	| (4) Chart-1 section for `iotaGm_range_isOpen` — `pullback.lift` over `pointOfVec` factorization | BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER | informational |
405	
406	## Recommendation
407	
408	**The iter-181 Lane B task_result was over-pessimistic on the upstream
409	gap.** Mathlib already ships the Spec-level intersection iso
410	`Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` with three `simp`-lemma companions
411	(`pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι`, `pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst`,
412	`pullbackAwayιIso_inv_snd`) that directly pin every projection. The
413	iter-182+ plan should:
414	
415	1. **Drop step 1 of the iter-181 plan** ("build `Away_X0_X1_iso`"). It's
416	   gratuitous — `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` IS the iso, packaged at the Spec
417	   level where it's needed.
418	2. **Keep step 2** ("build the source-side bridge `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1)
419	   ≅ Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 · X 1)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`"). This is the
420	   outer pasting iso that mirrors `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` with the
421	   merged generator `X 0 * X 1`. The key step inside the pasting chain
422	   is `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso` (no longer a sorry).
423	3. **For step 3** (the ring-level identity `λ · u = (1/t) · λ`), use
424	   `Proj.pullbackAwayιIso_inv_fst` / `_inv_snd` to push both sides of
425	   the cocycle through the intersection iso, then reduce to a
426	   `congrArg Spec.map` on the underlying ring-level equation. The
427	   residual algebra (`t · u = 1` in the merged Localization.Away) is
428	   `IsLocalization.Away.mul_invSelf` (≤5 LOC).
429	4. **For `iotaGm_range_isOpen`** (Route 3), use the same chart-1
430	   factorization machinery the iter-181 task_result correctly identified
431	   (`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict` + `pullback.lift` + the
432	   `Cover.ι_glueMorphisms` reduction). This is project-side
433	   `BUILD_PROJECT_HELPER` work but does NOT depend on the intersection
434	   ring — only on chart-1 of the cover.
435	
436	**Bonus** (out of scope for iter-182's helper budget but ~30-40 LOC):
437	the same chart-1 factorization (via `r_0` instead of `r_1`, since
438	`zeroPt`'s `v 1 = 1` is the chart-1 generator) closes
439	`gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` (L420 of `GmScaling.lean`).
440	
441	## What did NOT need to change
442	
443	- `Proj.affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan` choice — already canonical.
444	- `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` + `_algebraMap` — already axiom-clean
445	  iter-174 and reused in the new cross01 body recipe.
446	- `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` — already axiom-clean iter-173 and reusable
447	  uniform-in-`f` for the `X 0 * X 1` generator (degree 2 instance via
448	  `MvPolynomial.IsHomogeneous.mul`).
449	- `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` — axiom-clean iter-167; consumed by
450	  the chart maps that the cross01 body unfolds in step (a) of Recipe 2.
451	
452	## Reversal trigger
453	
454	If the `gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` body of Recipe 1 cannot
455	be made to type-check at the `pullback.congrHom`/`pullbackAwayιIso_hom_awayι`
456	step (the `Spec.map` of the merged generator and the merged `awayι`
457	endpoint must defeq-align), iter-183 falls back to **two separate
458	isos** — `pullback ((cover).f 0) ((cover).f 1) ≅ pullback q (awayι (X 0 * X 1))`
459	and `pullback q (awayι (X 0 * X 1)) ≅ Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X 0 * X 1)) ⊗ GmRing)`
460	— rather than fusing them into one chained iso. Same total LOC; less
461	risk of unification timeouts.

12	# Genus-`0` base objects (Stratum 4): the `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_×` and product-stability instances
13	
14	This file is **Stratum 4** of the four-stratum split of the legacy
15	`AlgebraicJacobian.Genus0BaseObjects` (iter-175 refactor `g0bo-split`). It ships:

27	
28	Upstream strata: `BareScheme`, `ChartIso`, `Points`.

42	/-! ### Recipe 1 helpers (iter-184 Lane B, `analogies/gmscaling-projection-idiom.md`):
43	project-side simp helpers for `pullback.map ≫ pullback.fst/snd`.
44	
45	Mathlib's `pullback.lift_fst` / `pullback.lift_snd` are `@[reassoc]` ONLY, not
46	`@[simp]`, so the abbrev `pullback.map _ _ _ _ i₁ i₂ i₃ _ _ ≫ pullback.fst _ _`
47	is not collapsed by `simp` even though the equation holds definitionally. These
48	two local simp lemmas unblock projection-chain collapse for
49	`gmScalingP1_cover_intersection_X_iso` whose STEP 4 uses
50	`asIso (pullback.map …)`. Candidate for upstream Mathlib contribution. -/
51	
52	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
53	lemma pullback_map_fst_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
54	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
55	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
56	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
57	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
58	        Limits.pullback.fst g₁ g₂ =
59	      Limits.pullback.fst f₁ f₂ ≫ i₁ :=
60	  Limits.pullback.lift_fst _ _ _
61	
62	@[reassoc (attr := simp)]
63	lemma pullback_map_snd_proj {C : Type*} [Category C] {W X Y Z S T : C}
64	    (f₁ : W ⟶ S) (f₂ : X ⟶ S) [Limits.HasPullback f₁ f₂] (g₁ : Y ⟶ T)
65	    (g₂ : Z ⟶ T) [Limits.HasPullback g₁ g₂] (i₁ : W ⟶ Y) (i₂ : X ⟶ Z) (i₃ : S ⟶ T)
66	    (eq₁ : f₁ ≫ i₃ = i₁ ≫ g₁) (eq₂ : f₂ ≫ i₃ = i₂ ≫ g₂) :
67	    Limits.pullback.map f₁ f₂ g₁ g₂ i₁ i₂ i₃ eq₁ eq₂ ≫
68	        Limits.pullback.snd g₁ g₂ =
69	      Limits.pullback.snd f₁ f₂ ≫ i₂ :=
70	  Limits.pullback.lift_snd _ _ _

72	/-! ### Chart-bridge: `Proj.awayι ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away _ _))`
73	
74	The helper below is the iter-173 `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe step (a)
75	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). Used by `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (below the `gmScalingP1_cover`
76	definition). -/
77	
78	/-- **`Proj.awayι 𝒜 f _ _ ≫ PLB.hom = Spec.map (algebraMap kbar (Away 𝒜 f))`** for any
79	homogeneous element `f` of positive degree.
80	
81	Generic in the element `f` AND in the degree `m` so we can apply it to either
82	`(![X 0, X 1]) i` (the actual chart input from `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover.f i`,
83	which uses `m = (![1, 1]) i`) or `X i` (which uses `m = 1`). A pure rewrite chasing
84	`awayι_toSpecZero` + `Spec.map_comp` + the `algebraKbarAway` defeq. -/
85	private lemma awayι_comp_PLB_hom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar]
86	    {m : ℕ} (hm : 0 < m)
87	    (f : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) (hf : f ∈ projectiveLineBarGrading kbar m) :
88	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f hf hm ≫
89	      (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
90	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
91	      (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) f))) := by
92	  change Proj.awayι _ _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
93	  rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.awayι_toSpecZero, ← Spec.map_comp,
94	    ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp]
95	
96	/-- **`ℙ¹` is reduced.** Closed axiom-clean iter-168 via `IsReduced.of_openCover` over
97	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover`; each chart `Spec (HomogeneousLocalization.Away 𝒜 (X_i))`
98	is a domain because the canonical `val`-injection into `Localization.Away (X_i)` (a
99	localization of `k̄[X_0, X_1]` at a non-zero-divisor, hence a domain) factors through
100	`Function.Injective.isDomain`. Exported here for Lane B (replaces its inline `haveI hP1red`).
101	
102	**Moved to this location iter-193 Lane B**: previously declared after the
103	`gmScalingP1_*` block; relocated so the cocycle proof
104	`gmScalingP1_chart_agreement_cross01` can pick up the
105	`IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left` instance needed by Substrate 1
106	(`IsClosedImmersion.lift_iff_range_subset`). -/
107	instance projectiveLineBar_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
108	    IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).left := by
109	  change IsReduced (ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar)
110	  -- Strategy: `IsReduced.of_openCover` over `projectiveLineBarAffineCover.openCover`.
111	  -- Each chart is `Spec(.of (Away 𝒜 (X i)))`; `IsReduced (Spec R)` if `R` is reduced.
112	  -- `Away 𝒜 (X i)` is a domain (and hence reduced) because it embeds via `val_injective`
113	  -- into `Localization.Away (X i)`, which is a localization of `MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar`
114	  -- (a domain) at a non-zero-divisor — hence a domain.
115	  haveI : ∀ i : Fin 2, IsReduced ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.X i) := by
116	    intro i
117	    change IsReduced (Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
118	        (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
119	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))))
120	    haveI : IsDomain (Localization.Away ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar),
121	        MvPolynomial.X 1] : Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
122	      fin_cases i <;>
123	        exact IsLocalization.isDomain_localization
124	          (powers_le_nonZeroDivisors_of_noZeroDivisors (MvPolynomial.X_ne_zero _))
125	    haveI : IsDomain (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
126	        ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
127	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)) := by
128	      refine Function.Injective.isDomain
129	        (algebraMap
130	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
131	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
132	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
133	          (Localization.Away
134	            ((![(MvPolynomial.X 0 : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar), MvPolynomial.X 1] :
135	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))) ?_
136	      intro x y h
137	      exact HomogeneousLocalization.val_injective _ h
138	    infer_instance
139	  exact IsReduced.of_openCover _ (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover

141	/-! ### (D) The `𝔾_m`-scaling action `σ_× : ℙ¹ × 𝔾_m ⟶ ℙ¹`
142	
143	`gmScalingP1` is a *bare* `Over (Spec (.of kbar))`-morphism (the analogist D3 verdict:
144	no `IsAction`/`MulAction`-style typeclass at scheme level — Mathlib has no such precedent;
145	the rigidity consumer needs only the bare morphism + the named fixed-point lemma).
146	
147	Chartwise definition: on `𝔸¹ × 𝔾_m` (target chart `D₊(X₀)` of `ℙ¹`), the morphism is
148	the polynomial map `(x, λ) ↦ λx`; near `∞` (target chart `D₊(X₁)`, coordinate `u = 1/x`),
149	the target coordinate `1/(λx) = u/λ` is regular because `λ ∈ 𝔾_m` is invertible. The two
150	chart-restrictions agree on `(𝔸¹ ∖ {0}) × 𝔾_m`, so they glue via
151	`AlgebraicGeometry.Scheme.Cover.glueMorphisms`.
152	
153	The companion lemma `gmScalingP1_collapse_at_zero` exposes the load-bearing fixed-point
154	property `σ_×(0, λ) = 0` for all `λ ∈ 𝔾_m`, packaged as the `W`-axis-collapse hypothesis
155	that `hom_additive_decomp_of_rigidity` (Cor 1.5) consumes. -/
156	
157	/-- **Chart-1 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
158	coord `u = X 0 / X 1 ↦ u ⊗ λ`, where `λ = X () ∈ GmRing kbar`. Uses
159	`MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` with the algebra-map `kbar →+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar ⊗[kbar] GmRing`
160	(target carrier carries `Algebra kbar` because both factors do). Axiom-clean. -/
161	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart1_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
162	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
163	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
164	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
165	      (algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) (MvPolynomial.X ())))
166	
167	/-- **Chart-0 ring map for `σ_×`** at the `MvPolynomial Unit kbar`-level: sends the affine
168	coord `t = X 1 / X 0 ↦ t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The `λ⁻¹` is `IsLocalization.Away.invSelf (X ())` in
169	`GmRing kbar = Localization.Away (X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar)`. Axiom-clean. -/
170	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart0_ringMap (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
171	    MvPolynomial Unit kbar →+* TensorProduct kbar (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar) :=
172	  MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
173	    (fun _ => (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
174	      (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
175	        (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) :
176	        GmRing kbar))
177	
178	/-- **The pullback open cover of `(ℙ¹ ⊗ 𝔾_m).left`** along `pullback.fst`, indexed by
179	the 2-chart cover `projectiveLineBarAffineCover` of `ProjectiveLineBar.left`. The `i`-th
180	component is `pullback (pullback.fst PLB.hom Gm.hom) (Proj.awayι 𝒜 (X i) …)`. -/
181	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
182	    ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).left.OpenCover :=
183	  (projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.pullback₁
184	    (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
185	
186	/-- **The chart-`i` source of `gmScalingP1_cover` is
187	`Spec ((Away 𝒜 ((![X 0, X 1]) i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing kbar)`.**
188	
189	Built by composing `pullbackSymmetry`, `pullbackRightPullbackFstIso`, the
190	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` rewrite via `pullback.congrHom`, and `pullbackSpecIso`. Mirrors the
191	Mathlib precedent `OpenCover.pullbackCoverAffineRefinementObjIso`
192	(`Mathlib.AlgebraicGeometry.Cover.Open:160-166`). Used by `gmScalingP1_chart`.
193	
194	**Uniform-in-`i` (iter-179 refactor `cover-bridge-uniform-i`):** the target type carries
195	`((![X 0, X 1]) i)` rather than `MvPolynomial.X i`. The hoisted helpers
196	`projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg`/`_hm` together with the m-generalised
197	`awayι_comp_PLB_hom` keep the bridge chain syntactic so `pullbackSpecIso` applies
198	generically — no `match`-on-`i` decoration. -/
199	private noncomputable def gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
200	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ≅
201	      Spec (CommRingCat.of
202	        (TensorProduct kbar
203	          (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
204	            ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
205	              Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i))
206	          (GmRing kbar))) :=
207	  pullbackSymmetry _ _ ≪≫
208	    pullbackRightPullbackFstIso _ _ _ ≪≫
209	    pullback.congrHom
210	      (awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_hm i)
211	        ((![MvPolynomial.X 0, MvPolynomial.X 1] :
212	          Fin 2 → MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) i)
213	        (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i))
214	      (show (Gm kbar).hom =
215	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar (GmRing kbar))) from rfl) ≪≫
216	    pullbackSpecIso kbar _ (GmRing kbar)
217	
218	/-- **The chart-`i` scheme morphism** `(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme`
219	defining `σ_×` on the `i`-th chart. On chart-1 (target `D₊(X 1)`), the affine coord
220	`u = X 0 / X 1` is sent to `u ⊗ λ`; on chart-0 (target `D₊(X 0)`), `t = X 1 / X 0` is sent
221	to `t ⊗ λ⁻¹`. The scheme map is built from `gmScalingP1_chart{0,1}_ringMap` (the chart-side
222	ring maps) via `pullbackSpecIso` + (the chart-ring iso
223	`HomogeneousLocalization.Away ≃+* MvPolynomial Unit kbar`) + `Proj.awayι`.
224	
225	**Status (iter-173):** body landed via the `mathlib-analogist chart-bridge173` recipe
226	(`analogies/chart-bridge.md`). The bridge `gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso` (above) identifies the
227	source with `Spec ((Away 𝒜 (X i)) ⊗[kbar] GmRing)`. The chart-ring iso
228	`homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso` plus a chart-`i`-specific `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom` produces
229	the ring map `Away 𝒜 (X i) →+* Away 𝒜 (X i) ⊗ GmRing`, then `Proj.awayι` lands the
230	result in `ProjectiveLineBarScheme`. -/
231	noncomputable def gmScalingP1_chart (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
232	    (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).X i ⟶ ProjectiveLineBarScheme kbar :=
233	  (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫
234	    Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom
235	      ((MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom (algebraMap kbar _)
236	          (fun _ : Unit =>
237	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away.isLocalizationElem
238	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar i)
239	                (projectiveLineBarAffineCover_fDeg kbar (otherFin i))) ⊗ₜ[kbar]
240	              (match i with
241	               | ⟨0, _⟩ =>
242	                  (IsLocalization.Away.invSelf
243	                    (MvPolynomial.X () : MvPolynomial Unit kbar) : GmRing kbar)
244	               | ⟨1, _⟩ =>
245	                  algebraMap (MvPolynomial Unit kbar) (GmRing kbar)
246	                    (MvPolynomial.X ())))).comp
247	        (homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso kbar i).toRingHom)) ≫
248	    Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
249	      (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
250	      (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos
251	
252	set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false in
253	/-- **Shared per-chart helper for `gmScalingP1`** (iter-174 Sub-task A per
254	`analogies/chart-bridge-shared-helper.md` Decision 3). On the `i`-th chart of the
255	`gmScalingP1_cover`, the composition `gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ PLB.hom` agrees with
256	`(gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom`.
257	
258	This is the per-chart certificate used by `gmScalingP1_over_coherence` (via
259	`Scheme.Cover.hom_ext` + `Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc`).
260	
261	**Status (iter-180):** body retired axiom-clean via the empirically-verified
262	`set_option backward.isDefEq.respectTransparency false` recipe of
263	`analogies/pullbackspeciso-bypass.md` (Decision 4). The option collapses the
264	`Algebra.compHom`-driven heartbeat sink on the `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` rewrite

266	private lemma gmScalingP1_chart_PLB_eq (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (i : Fin 2) :
267	    gmScalingP1_chart kbar i ≫ (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom =
268	      (gmScalingP1_cover kbar).f i ≫ ((ProjectiveLineBar kbar) ⊗ Gm kbar).hom := by
269	  -- Stage 1 (iter-179): unfold the chart map, apply `awayι_comp_PLB_hom` after a
270	  -- type-realigning `change`, and collapse the right-hand `Spec.map` chain into
271	  -- `Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))` via
272	  -- `homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap` + `MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_comp_C`.
273	  unfold gmScalingP1_chart
274	  have h := awayι_comp_PLB_hom kbar (m := 1) Nat.one_pos
275	    (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
276	    (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i)
277	  change (gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso kbar i).hom ≫ _ ≫
278	      ((Proj.awayι (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
279	          (MvPolynomial.X i : MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar)
280	          (MvPolynomial.isHomogeneous_X kbar i) Nat.one_pos :
281	        Spec (CommRingCat.of (HomogeneousLocalization.Away
282	          (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar) (MvPolynomial.X i))) ⟶
283	          Proj (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)) ≫
284	        (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom) = _
285	  rw [h, ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
286	    homogeneousLocalizationAwayIso_algebraMap, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq,
287	    MvPolynomial.eval₂Hom_comp_C]
288	  -- Stage 2 (iter-180 fix): the `respectTransparency` option lets the
289	  -- `pullbackSpecIso_hom_base` simp lemma fire on the buried iso chain;
290	  -- it produces `pullback.fst _ _ ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar Away_i)` in place
291	  -- of `(pullbackSpecIso).hom ≫ Spec.map (algMap kbar (Away_i ⊗ GmRing))`.
292	  -- The follow-up simps collapse the `pullback.congrHom`-wrapped map into a
293	  -- bare `pullback.fst (cover.f i ≫ PLB.hom) Gm.hom`.
294	  unfold gmScalingP1_cover_X_iso
295	  simp only [Iso.trans_hom, Category.assoc, pullbackSpecIso_hom_base,
296	    pullback.congrHom_hom, pullback.lift_fst_assoc, Category.id_comp]

299	  change (pullbackSymmetry (pullback.fst (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom)
300	        ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i)).hom ≫
301	      (pullbackRightPullbackFstIso (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom (Gm kbar).hom
302	          ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i)).hom ≫
303	        pullback.fst
304	            ((projectiveLineBarAffineCover kbar).openCover.f i ≫
305	              (ProjectiveLineBar kbar).hom)
306	            (Gm kbar).hom ≫
307	          Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap kbar
308	            (HomogeneousLocalization.Away (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)

119:`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/

/-- **Helper: construct a `k̄`-point of `ProjectiveLineBar kbar`** from an evaluation vector
`v : Fin 2 → kbar` with at least one unit coordinate. The underlying scheme map is
`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation; the section condition chases through
`fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero` + `IsScalarTower kbar (𝒜 0) MvPoly` collapse to
`MvPolynomial.C` + `MvPolynomial.eval_C` + `toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv`. -/
private noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec
    (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] (v : Fin 2 → kbar) (i : Fin 2) (hi : IsUnit (v i)) :
    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
  Over.homMk
    (Proj.fromOfGlobalSections (projectiveLineBarGrading kbar)
      (ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v)
      (ProjectiveLineBar.irrelevant_map_eq_top kbar v i hi)) <| by
    -- Section condition: fromOfGlobalSections ≫ ProjectiveLineBar.hom = 𝟙.
    haveI : IsScalarTower kbar ↥(projectiveLineBarGrading kbar 0)
        (MvPolynomial (Fin 2) kbar) :=
      IsScalarTower.of_algebraMap_eq fun _ => rfl
    -- Expose the structure morphism's composition shape.
    change Proj.fromOfGlobalSections _ _ _ ≫ Proj.toSpecZero _ ≫ Spec.map _ = _
    -- Combine via `fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero`, then unify the two Spec.maps.
    rw [← Category.assoc, Proj.fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero, Category.assoc,
      ← Spec.map_comp, ← CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, RingHom.comp_assoc,
      ← IsScalarTower.algebraMap_eq kbar, MvPolynomial.algebraMap_eq]
    -- The inner composition `(evalIntoGlobal v) ∘ C` collapses to `(ΓSpecIso).inv.hom`.
    -- Build a CommRingCat-level equation, then apply `Spec.map` and finish.
    have hcc : CommRingCat.ofHom
        ((ProjectiveLineBar.evalIntoGlobal kbar v).comp MvPolynomial.C) =
        (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
      apply CommRingCat.hom_ext
      ext r
      change (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv.hom
          ((MvPolynomial.eval v) (MvPolynomial.C r)) = _
      rw [MvPolynomial.eval_C]
    calc _ = (Spec (CommRingCat.of kbar)).toSpecΓ ≫
            Spec.map (Scheme.ΓSpecIso (CommRingCat.of kbar)).inv := by
            exact congrArg _ (congrArg Spec.map hcc)
      _ = _ := AlgebraicGeometry.toSpecΓ_SpecMap_ΓSpecIso_inv _

/-- The `k̄`-point `0 = [0 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section of `ProjectiveLineBar.hom` via
`Proj.fromOfGlobalSections` of the evaluation `X₀ ↦ 0`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.zeroPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 0 else 1) 1 (by simp)

/-- The `k̄`-point `1 = [1 : 1] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 1`. -/
noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.onePt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun _ => 1) 0 (by simp)

/-- The `k̄`-point `∞ = [1 : 0] ∈ ℙ¹`, encoded as a section via the evaluation
`X₀ ↦ 1`, `X₁ ↦ 0`. -/
noncomputable def ProjectiveLineBar.inftyPt (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    𝟙_ (Over (Spec (.of kbar))) ⟶ ProjectiveLineBar kbar :=
  ProjectiveLineBar.pointOfVec kbar (fun i => if i = 0 then 1 else 0) 0 (by simp)

/-! ### (B) The additive group `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` -/

/-- **The additive group `𝔾_a = 𝔸¹` over `Spec k̄` as an underlying scheme.** This is the
affine line `AffineSpace.{u} (ULift.{u} (Fin 1)) (Spec (.of kbar))`. It is affine, locally of finite
presentation, and reduced (its global sections are `MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain). -/
def GaScheme (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Scheme :=
  AffineSpace.{u} (ULift.{u} (Fin 1)) (Spec (.of kbar))

/-- The natural `Over (Spec (.of kbar))` instance on `GaScheme` (via
`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.over`). -/
instance gaScheme_canOver (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    (GaScheme kbar).Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
  inferInstanceAs ((AffineSpace.{u} (ULift.{u} (Fin 1)) (Spec (.of kbar))).Over (Spec (.of kbar)))

/-- **The additive group object `𝔾_a` over `Spec k̄` as an object of
`Over (Spec (.of kbar))`.** -/
abbrev Ga (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Over (Spec (.of kbar)) :=
  (GaScheme kbar).asOver (Spec (.of kbar))

/-- **`𝔾_a` is an affine morphism over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
`AlgebraicGeometry.AffineSpace.instIsAffineHomOverSchemeInferInstanceOverClass`. -/
instance ga_isAffineHom (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    IsAffineHom (Ga kbar).hom :=
  inferInstanceAs (IsAffineHom (AffineSpace.{u} (ULift.{u} (Fin 1)) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘
    (Spec (.of kbar))))

/-- **`𝔾_a` is locally of finite presentation over `Spec k̄`.** FREE from
`AffineSpace.instLocallyOfFinitePresentation…OfFinite` (the index `Fin 1` is finite). -/
instance ga_locallyOfFinitePresentation (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] :
    LocallyOfFinitePresentation (Ga kbar).hom :=
  inferInstanceAs (LocallyOfFinitePresentation
    (AffineSpace.{u} (ULift.{u} (Fin 1)) (Spec (.of kbar)) ↘ Spec (.of kbar)))

/-- **`𝔾_a`'s underlying scheme is reduced.** Since the global sections are
`MvPolynomial (Fin 1) k̄`, a domain over a field, the affine scheme is reduced. The proof
transfers `IsReduced (Spec (.of (MvPolynomial _ _)))` (free for any reduced ring) across
`AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine`. -/
instance ga_isReduced (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : IsReduced (Ga kbar).left :=
  isReduced_of_isOpenImmersion (AffineSpace.isoOfIsAffine (ULift.{u} (Fin 1)) _).hom

/-! ### (C) The multiplicative group `𝔾_m` over `Spec k̄` -/

/-- **The ring `k̄[t, t⁻¹] = Localization.Away t`**, where `t = X () : MvPolynomial Unit k̄`.
This is the global-sections ring of `𝔾_m`. -/
abbrev GmRing (kbar : Type u) [Field kbar] : Type u :=

variable (f : A →+* Γ(X, ⊤)) (hf : (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant 𝒜).toIdeal.map f = ⊤)

/-- Given a graded ring `A` and a map `f : A →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` such that the image of the
irrelevant ideal under `f` generates the whole ring, the set of `D(f(r))` for homogeneous `r`
of positive degree forms an open cover on `X`. -/
def openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop : X.OpenCover :=
  X.openCoverOfIsOpenCover (fun ir : Σ' i r, 0 < i ∧ r ∈ 𝒜 i ↦
    X.basicOpen (f ir.2.1)) (by
    classical
    have H : Ideal.span (Set.range fun x : Σ' i r, 0 < i ∧ r ∈ 𝒜 i ↦ x.2.1) =
        (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant 𝒜).toIdeal := by
      apply le_antisymm
      · rw [Ideal.span_le, Set.range_subset_iff]
        rintro ⟨i, r, hi0, hri⟩
        simp [-ZeroMemClass.coe_eq_zero,
          DirectSum.decompose_of_mem_ne 𝒜 hri hi0.ne']
      · intro x hx
        rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose 𝒜 x]
        refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun c hc ↦ ?_
        have : c ≠ 0 := by contrapose hc; simpa [hc] using hx
        exact Ideal.subset_span ⟨⟨c, _, this.bot_lt, by simp⟩, rfl⟩
    ext1
    apply compl_injective
    simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_iSup, Set.compl_iUnion, ← Scheme.zeroLocus_singleton,
      ← Scheme.zeroLocus_iUnion, Set.iUnion_singleton_eq_range, TopologicalSpace.Opens.coe_top,
      Set.compl_univ]
    rw [← Scheme.zeroLocus_span, Set.range_comp', ← Ideal.map_span, H, hf]
    simp)

set_option backward.defeqAttrib.useBackward true in
/-- Given a graded ring `A` and a map `f : A →+* Γ(X, ⊤)` such that the image of the
irrelevant ideal under `f` generates the whole ring, we can construct a map `X ⟶ Proj 𝒜`. -/
def fromOfGlobalSections : X ⟶ Proj 𝒜 := by
  refine (openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop 𝒜 f hf).glueMorphisms
    (fun ri ↦ toBasicOpenOfGlobalSections 𝒜 f rfl ri.2.2.1 ri.2.2.2 ≫ Scheme.Opens.ι _) ?_
  rintro x y
  let e : pullback ((openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop 𝒜 f hf).f x)
      ((openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop 𝒜 f hf).f y) ≅ (X.basicOpen (f (x.snd.fst * y.snd.fst))) :=
    (isPullback_opens_inf _ _).isoPullback.symm ≪≫ X.isoOfEq (by simp)
  rw [← cancel_epi e.inv]
  trans toBasicOpenOfGlobalSections 𝒜 f rfl (Nat.add_pos_left x.2.2.1 y.1)
    (SetLike.mul_mem_graded x.2.2.2 y.2.2.2) ≫ (Scheme.Opens.ι _)
  · simpa [e, openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop, Scheme.isoOfEq_inv] using
      homOfLE_toBasicOpenOfGlobalSections_ι _ _ rfl rfl y.2.2.2
  · simpa [e, openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop, Scheme.isoOfEq_inv] using
      (homOfLE_toBasicOpenOfGlobalSections_ι _ _ (mul_comm _ _) (add_comm _ _) x.2.2.2).symm

lemma fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen {r : A} {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (hr : r ∈ 𝒜 n) :
    fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 f hf ⁻¹ᵁ basicOpen 𝒜 r = X.basicOpen (f r) := by
  apply le_antisymm
  · intro x hx
    obtain ⟨i, x, rfl⟩ := (openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop 𝒜 f hf).exists_eq x
    rw [← SetLike.mem_coe] at hx -- TODO : mem version of TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe
    simp only [TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe, Set.mem_preimage, SetLike.mem_coe,
      ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, fromOfGlobalSections, Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms] at hx
    simp only [openCoverOfMapIrrelevantEqTop,
      toBasicOpenOfGlobalSections, Scheme.isoOfEq_inv, Category.assoc, basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_ι] at hx
    simp only [Scheme.Hom.comp_base, Scheme.homOfLE_base, homOfLE_leOfHom, TopCat.hom_comp,
      ContinuousMap.comp_assoc, ContinuousMap.comp_apply, morphismRestrict_base,
      TopologicalSpace.Opens.carrier_eq_coe] at hx
    rw [← SetLike.mem_coe, ← Set.mem_preimage, ← TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe,
      Proj.awayι_preimage_basicOpen (𝒜 := 𝒜) i.2.2.2 i.2.2.1 hr hn,
      ← Set.mem_preimage, ← TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe, ← Function.Injective.mem_set_image
      (Spec.map (CommRingCat.ofHom (algebraMap Γ(X, ⊤) _))).isOpenEmbedding.injective,
      ← Scheme.Hom.comp_apply, basicOpenIsoSpecAway, IsOpenImmersion.isoOfRangeEq_hom_fac] at hx
    rw [← SetLike.mem_coe, ← Scheme.toSpecΓ_preimage_basicOpen, TopologicalSpace.Opens.map_coe,
        Set.mem_preimage]
    refine Set.mem_of_subset_of_mem (Set.image_subset_iff.mpr ?_) hx
    change PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen _ ≤ PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen _
    simp only [CommRingCat.ofHom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_comp, CommRingCat.hom_ofHom,
      RingHom.coe_comp, Function.comp_apply, HomogeneousLocalization.algebraMap_apply,
      HomogeneousLocalization.Away.val_mk, Localization.mk_eq_mk', IsLocalization.map_mk', map_pow,
      PrimeSpectrum.basicOpen_le_basicOpen_iff, IsLocalization.mk'_mem_iff]
    exact Ideal.pow_mem_of_mem _ (Ideal.le_radical (Ideal.mem_span_singleton_self _)) _ i.2.2.1
  · intro x hx

lemma fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict {r : A} {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (hr : r ∈ 𝒜 n) :
    (fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 f hf) ∣_ (basicOpen 𝒜 r) =

lemma fromOfGlobalSections_resLE {r : A} {n : ℕ} (hn : 0 < n) (hr : r ∈ 𝒜 n) :
    (fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 f hf).resLE _ _
      (fromOfGlobalSections_preimage_basicOpen _ _ _ hn hr).ge =
      toBasicOpenOfGlobalSections 𝒜 f rfl hn hr := by
  rw [← (Iso.inv_comp_eq _).mpr (fromOfGlobalSections_morphismRestrict 𝒜 f hf hn hr),
    ← Scheme.Hom.resLE_eq_morphismRestrict]

lemma fromOfGlobalSections_toSpecZero
    (f : A →+* Γ(X, ⊤)) (hf : (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant 𝒜).toIdeal.map f = ⊤) :
    fromOfGlobalSections 𝒜 f hf ≫ toSpecZero 𝒜 =

  simp only [fromOfGlobalSections, toBasicOpenOfGlobalSections, CommRingCat.ofHom_comp,
    Category.assoc, Scheme.Cover.ι_glueMorphisms_assoc, basicOpenIsoSpec_inv_ι_assoc,
    awayι_toSpecZero, Iso.inv_comp_eq]

    Scheme.openCoverOfIsOpenCover_f, Scheme.isoOfEq_hom_ι_assoc, ← morphismRestrict_ι_assoc]

noncomputable
def affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan {ι : Type*} (f : ι → A) {m : ι → ℕ}
    (f_deg : ∀ i, f i ∈ 𝒜 (m i)) (hm : ∀ i, 0 < m i)
    (hf : (HomogeneousIdeal.irrelevant 𝒜).toIdeal ≤ Ideal.span (Set.range f)) :
    (Proj 𝒜).AffineOpenCover where
  I₀ := ι
  X i := .of (Away 𝒜 (f i))
  f i := awayι 𝒜 (f i) (f_deg i) (hm i)
  idx x := (mem_iSup.mp ((iSup_basicOpen_eq_top 𝒜 f hf).ge (Set.mem_univ x))).choose
  covers x := by
    change x ∈ (awayι 𝒜 _ _ _).opensRange
    rw [opensRange_awayι]
    exact (mem_iSup.mp ((iSup_basicOpen_eq_top 𝒜 f hf).ge (Set.mem_univ x))).choose_spec

/-- `Proj A` is covered by `Spec (A_f)₀` for all homogeneous elements of positive degree. -/
@[simps! f] noncomputable
def affineOpenCover : (Proj 𝒜).AffineOpenCover :=
  affineOpenCoverOfIrrelevantLESpan 𝒜
    (ι := Σ i : PNat, 𝒜 i) (m := fun i ↦ i.1) (fun i ↦ i.2) (fun i ↦ i.2.2) (fun i ↦ i.1.2) <| by
  classical
  intro z hz
  rw [← DirectSum.sum_support_decompose 𝒜 z]
  refine Ideal.sum_mem _ fun c hc ↦ if hc0 : c = 0 then ?_ else
    Ideal.subset_span ⟨⟨⟨c, Nat.pos_iff_ne_zero.mpr hc0⟩, _⟩, rfl⟩
  convert! Ideal.zero_mem _
  subst hc0
  exact hz

end basicOpen

/-- The stalk of `Proj A` at `x` is the degree `0` part of the localization of `A` at `x`. -/

def stalkIso (x : Proj 𝒜) :
